provinces.name: Siem Reap
provinces.tagline: An Eco-Tourism and Cultural Heritage Escape
provinces.description: Siem Reap is the historic heart of the Khmer Empire (9th–15th centuries) and a land named to commemorate a 15th-century victory over Siamese forces. This region served as a majestic civilization center, leaving behind world-class heritage sites like Angkor Wat and the Bayon Temple. Today, it stands as Cambodia's premier travel destination and the ultimate symbol of the nation's soul.

provinces.name: Kampot
provinces.tagline: A Riverside, Mountain, and Coastal Retreat — Home of Kampot Pepper and Salt
provinces.description: A riverside province famous for world-class Kampot pepper, colonial architecture, and relaxed countryside views. Kampot sits along the Preaek Tuek Chhu River and is the perfect slow-travel escape.

provinces.name: Kep
provinces.tagline: A Coastal Charm and Historical Seaside Escapade — Home of Crab and Pepper
provinces.description: A quiet coastal retreat known for fresh seafood, especially Kep crab, and peaceful beaches. Once a French colonial resort town, Kep offers a unique blend of history and tranquility.

provinces.name: Sihanoukville
provinces.tagline: A Vibrant Gateway and Pristine Island Escape — Home of Islands and Port
provinces.description: A cultural hub with well-preserved French colonial buildings, local art, and the unique Bamboo Train experience. Sihanoukville is also the gateway to Cambodia's most stunning tropical islands.

provinces.name: Mondulkiri
provinces.tagline: An Eco-Tourism Escape to the Mountains
provinces.description: A mountainous province known for cool climate, rolling hills, waterfalls, and eco-tourism experiences with elephants and indigenous Bunong culture.

provinces.name: Koh Kong
provinces.description: A nature-rich destination featuring dense rainforests, eco-tourism, and access to the Cardamom Mountains. Koh Kong is ideal for adventure seekers and nature lovers.

places.name: Angkor Wat
places.tagline: Iconic ancient temple complex famous for its grand Khmer architecture, intricate carvings, and breathtaking sunrise views.

quickInfo.activities: Temple exploration, sunrise watching, photography, cycling tours, guided literary tours, and ancient exploration

places.about: Angkor Wat is a 12th-century temple complex and the largest religious monument in the world. Originally built as a Hindu temple, it later became a Buddhist site. Known for its iconic five towers and detailed carvings, it stands as a symbol of Cambodia's history and culture.
thingsToDo: Explore the main temple and surrounding ruins
Take photos of the iconic towers and reflections
Experience local culture (monk blessings, nearby villages)

food.name: Amok Fish
food.desc: A traditional Khmer curry made with fish in coconut milk, cooked in banana leaves. Sweet, creamy, and aromatic.

food.name: Lok Lak
food.desc: A popular stir-fried dish with marinated beef or chicken served over rice, topped with a fried egg.

food.name: Nom Banh Chok
food.desc: Coconut milk noodles from young coconut served chilled, known for its fresh hydration and refreshing taste.

places.name: Bayon Temple
places.tagline: The smiling face temple at the heart of Angkor Thom — a surreal forest of stone faces.
places.about: Bayon is a richly decorated Khmer temple at Angkor Thom, built in the late 12th or early 13th century. Its most distinctive feature is the multitude of serene and smiling stone faces on the many towers.
thingsToDo: Walk among the 216 giant stone faces
Explore the bas-reliefs depicting historical battles

places.name: Ta Prohm Temple
places.tagline: Where ancient stone meets the jungle — roots and ruins intertwined.
places.about: Ta Prohm is famous for the trees growing out of its ruins and for being one of the filming locations of Lara Croft: Tomb Raider. It gives a sense of what Angkor was like when European explorers first encountered it.

places.name: Zipline Ride in Angkor Jungle
places.tagline: An adventurous hilltop sanctuary offering 360-degree views of the Angkor plains.
places.about: Experience the thrill of ziplining above the Angkor jungle canopy. This eco-adventure offers breathtaking bird's-eye views of ancient temple ruins and the forested plains of Siem Reap.

places.name: Kulen Mountain National Park
places.tagline: The birthplace of the ancient Khmer Empire — sacred mountain with waterfalls and carved riverbeds.

quickInfo.activities: Hiking, waterfall swimming, visiting reclining Buddha

places.about: Kulen Mountain is considered sacred by Cambodians as the birthplace of the Khmer Empire. The mountain features a reclining Buddha carved from sandstone, hundreds of lingas carved into the river bed, and a beautiful waterfall.

places.name: Phnom Bok Temple
places.tagline: An adventurous hilltop sanctuary offering 360-degree views of the Angkor plains.
places.about: Phnom Bok is a hilltop temple featuring three towers dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu, and Brahma. The climb rewards visitors with sweeping views of the Cambodian plains and a sense of remote discovery.

places.name: Kampong Phluk Floating Village
places.tagline: A living floating village on the Tonle Sap lake — a unique way of life.
places.about: Kampong Phluk is a cluster of three villages on the Tonle Sap lake. Houses are built on stilts up to 6 meters high, and the village has a school, temple, and market all floating on the water.

places.name: Bokor Mountain
places.tagline: Historical 'ghost city' in the clouds — 1920s French colonial ruins, cool climate, and eerie landscapes.

quickInfo.activities: Ruins exploration, scenic drive, waterfall visit

places.about: Bokor Hill Station is an abandoned French colonial resort perched at 1,079m on Bokor Mountain. The eerie ghost town with its fog-covered ruins is one of Cambodia's most atmospheric experiences, now also hosting a modern casino resort.
thingsToDo: Explore the old French hill station ruins
See the black palace and old church

places.name: Tatai Waterfall
places.tagline: Experience the majestic 'Niagara of Cambodia' — pure mountain water cascades over massive rocks into a refreshing jungle paradise.
places.about: Tatai Waterfall is a stunning cascade on the Tatai River in Koh Kong province. Surrounded by lush jungle and clear water, it's the perfect spot for a refreshing swim in nature.

places.name: Koh Rong
places.tagline: The crown jewel of Cambodia's islands — 40km of white-sand beaches, turquoise waters, vibrant nightlife and total serenity.
places.about: Koh Rong is Cambodia's second largest island and one of Southeast Asia's most stunning tropical escapes. With 43km of coastline and 23 beaches, it offers party-goers the Koh Touch beach strip and seekers of serenity the quiet Long Set Beach.

places.name: Koh Tonsay (Rabbit Island)
places.tagline: Iconic peaceful tropical island famous for its white sand beaches and fresh seafood dining.
places.about: Koh Tonsay, or Rabbit Island, is a small paradise just a 20-minute boat ride from Kep. With a handful of bamboo bungalows, no electricity after dark, and incredible fresh seafood, it's a true off-grid tropical escape.
thingsToDo: Eat fresh crab and grilled fish

places.name: Koh Kong Krav
places.tagline: An untouched island sanctuary — seven secluded beaches, emerald lagoons, and majestic coastal cliffs.
places.about: Koh Kong Krav is Cambodia's hidden gem — a pristine uninhabited island featuring seven white-sand beaches, emerald lagoons, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and majestic coastal cliffs. A perfect off-the-beaten-path escape for true nature lovers.

places.name: Bousra Waterfall
places.tagline: A stunning three-level cascade and the pride of Mondulkiri — powerful flow, lush jungle setting, refreshing swimming spots.
places.about: Bousra Waterfall is a magnificent three-tiered waterfall in Mondulkiri province. Surrounded by lush evergreen forest, the falls cascade down into natural pools ideal for swimming. It's one of Cambodia's most impressive natural attractions.

places.name: Dak Dam Waterfall
places.tagline: A tall waterfall in a serene forested mountain area with large trees, cool and clear water — ideal for hiking and picnicking.
places.about: Dak Dam Waterfall is nestled in the highland forests of Mondulkiri. The tall, powerful cascade flows into a clear, cool pool surrounded by ancient trees — a perfect hidden retreat for nature and hiking enthusiasts.
thingsToDo: Swim in the cool cascade pool

places.name: King Island (Koh Sdach)
places.tagline: Step into a living piece of Cambodian history — ancient legends of royalty meet a resilient fishing community.
places.about: Koh Sdach (King Island) is a charming fishing community island in Koh Kong. Named after a legendary Cambodian king, it offers visitors an authentic glimpse into local island life, with fresh seafood and a handful of guesthouses.

places.name: Kep National Park
places.tagline: Iconic coastal jungle sanctuary famous for lush forest trails, diverse wildlife, and breathtaking ocean views.
places.about: Kep National Park is a small but spectacular forested area on the Kep peninsula. Its network of trails winds through dense jungle to viewpoints overlooking the Gulf of Thailand, with chances to spot wildlife including butterflies and forest birds.

places.name: Pub Street
places.tagline: Siem Reap's legendary nightlife strip — neon lights, street food, live music, and Cambodian cocktails.

quickInfo.activities: Bar hopping, street food, live music, cocktails

places.about: Pub Street is the vibrant heart of Siem Reap's nightlife. Lined with bars, restaurants, and street stalls, it comes alive after dark with live music, happy-hour deals, and the smell of grilled skewers. A must-visit evening experience for every traveller.
thingsToDo: Try a Khmer cocktail at a rooftop bar

food.name: Grilled Skewers
food.desc: Marinated chicken, pork, and shrimp grilled over charcoal — a street food staple.

food.name: Amok Fish
food.desc: Traditional Khmer curry cooked in banana leaves — rich, coconut, and fragrant.

places.name: Fair Trade Village
places.tagline: A community of artisans preserving traditional Khmer crafts — silk weaving, pottery, and wood carving.

quickInfo.activities: Craft workshops, silk weaving demo, souvenir shopping

places.about: The Fair Trade Village near Angkor Wat is a craft hub where local artisans hand-make traditional Khmer items — from silk scarves and lacquerware to silver jewellery and stone carvings. All purchases directly support the local community.

places.name: Phnom Krom Temple
places.tagline: A hilltop temple ruin with sweeping views over the Tonle Sap lake and the setting sun.

quickInfo.activities: Temple visit, sunset watching, lake views

places.about: Phnom Krom sits on a low hill at the southern end of Siem Reap, overlooking the Tonle Sap lake. The 10th-century temple features three towers dedicated to the Hindu Trinity — Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva — and offers stunning sunset views.
thingsToDo: Photograph the Tonle Sap lake below

places.name: Kampot Old Town
places.tagline: A charming riverside colonial town — French-era architecture, pepper farms, art cafes, and slow-travel vibes.

quickInfo.activities: River boat tour, cycling, pepper farm visit, café hopping

places.about: Kampot Old Town is a beautifully preserved French colonial riverside town. Wander pastel-coloured shophouses, visit a working Kampot pepper farm, take a sunset boat cruise on the Praek Tuek Chhu River, and enjoy the laid-back, artistic atmosphere.

food.name: Pepper Crab
food.desc: Fresh crab cooked with the world-famous Kampot green pepper — a local specialty you can't miss.

places.name: Kep Crab Market
places.tagline: Fresh crab, sea breezes, and seaside dining — the living heart of Kep's coastal culture.

quickInfo.activities: Fresh crab dining, market visit, waterfront walk

places.about: Kep's famous Crab Market is a sprawling waterfront bazaar where fishermen bring in fresh catches daily. Surrounded by seafood shacks on stilts over the water, visitors can choose their live crab and have it cooked to order with Kampot pepper.
thingsToDo: Choose and cook your own fresh crab
Walk the coastal promenade at sunset

food.name: Kampot Pepper Crab
food.desc: Live blue crab stir-fried with fresh Kampot green pepper — the signature dish of Kep.

food.name: Grilled Squid
food.desc: Whole squid grilled over charcoal and served with sweet chilli sauce.

places.name: Bunong Indigenous Village
places.tagline: Connect with Cambodia's indigenous Bunong people — traditional longhouses, forest wisdom, and ancient customs.

quickInfo.activities: Village tour, traditional ceremony, jungle walk, coffee farm

places.about: The Bunong are the indigenous highland people of Mondulkiri, with deep ties to the forest and a rich oral culture. Village visits offer a rare window into traditional longhouse life, animist ceremonies, hand-woven textiles, and the famous Bunong elephant relationship.
thingsToDo: Learn about elephant and forest traditions

places.name: Elephant Valley Project
places.tagline: Walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural highland habitat — Cambodia's most ethical elephant experience.

quickInfo.activities: Elephant walks, jungle trekking, waterfall swimming

places.about: The Elephant Valley Project is a sanctuary for rescued domestic elephants, allowing them to roam free across 1,500 hectares of highland forest. Visitors walk alongside the elephants through the jungle, observe their natural behaviour, and swim in waterfalls — all in an ethical, no-riding environment.
thingsToDo: Walk with elephants in the jungle

places.name: Cardamom Mountains
places.tagline: Southeast Asia's last great wilderness — ancient rainforest, rare wildlife, and untouched rivers.

quickInfo.activities: Jungle trekking, river kayaking, wildlife spotting, camping

places.about: The Cardamom Mountains are one of Southeast Asia's most important biodiversity hotspots, sheltering sun bears, clouded leopards, Irrawaddy dolphins, and thousands of plant species. The dense rainforest, clear rivers, and rugged terrain make it Cambodia's ultimate wilderness adventure.
thingsToDo: Spot rare wildlife with a ranger
